Interface ReactiveScriptingCommands

All Known Subinterfaces:
ReactiveClusterScriptingCommands
public interface ReactiveScriptingCommands
Redis Scripting commands executed using reactive infrastructure.
Since:
2.0
Author:
Mark Paluch, Christoph Strobl

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    <T> Flux<T>
    eval(ByteBuffer script, ReturnType returnType, int numKeys, ByteBuffer... keysAndArgs)
    Evaluate given script.
    <T> Flux<T>
    evalSha(String scriptSha, ReturnType returnType, int numKeys, ByteBuffer... keysAndArgs)
    Evaluate given scriptSha.
    default Mono<Boolean>
    scriptExists(String scriptSha)
    Check if given scriptSha exist in script cache.
    Flux<Boolean>
    scriptExists(List<String> scriptShas)
    Check if given scriptShas exist in script cache.
    Mono<String>
    scriptFlush()
    Flush lua script cache.
    Mono<String>
    scriptKill()
    Kill current lua script execution.
    Mono<String>
    scriptLoad(ByteBuffer script)
    Load lua script into scripts cache, without executing it.
    Execute the script by calling evalSha(String, ReturnType, int, ByteBuffer...).

  • Method Details

    • scriptFlush

      Mono<String> scriptFlush()
      Flush lua script cache.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SCRIPT FLUSH

    • scriptKill

      Mono<String> scriptKill()
      Kill current lua script execution.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SCRIPT KILL

    • scriptLoad

      Mono<String> scriptLoad(ByteBuffer script)
      Load lua script into scripts cache, without executing it.
      Execute the script by calling evalSha(String, ReturnType, int, ByteBuffer...).
      Parameters:
      script - must not be null.
      Returns:
      never null.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SCRIPT LOAD

    • scriptExists

      default Mono<Boolean> scriptExists(String scriptSha)
      Check if given scriptSha exist in script cache.
      Parameters:
      scriptSha - The sha1 of the script is present in script cache. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      a Mono indicating if script is present.

    • scriptExists

      Flux<Boolean> scriptExists(List<String> scriptShas)
      Check if given scriptShas exist in script cache.
      Parameters:
      scriptShas - must not be null.
      Returns:
      Flux emitting one entry per scriptSha in given List.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: SCRIPT EXISTS

    • eval

      <T> Flux<T> eval(ByteBuffer script, ReturnType returnType, int numKeys, ByteBuffer... keysAndArgs)
      Evaluate given script.
      Parameters:
      script - must not be null.
      returnType - must not be null. Using ReturnType.MULTI emits a List as-is instead of emitting the individual elements from the array response.
      numKeys -
      keysAndArgs - must not be null.
      Returns:
      never null.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: EVAL

    • evalSha

      <T> Flux<T> evalSha(String scriptSha, ReturnType returnType, int numKeys, ByteBuffer... keysAndArgs)
      Evaluate given scriptSha.
      Parameters:
      scriptSha - must not be null.
      returnType - must not be null. Using ReturnType.MULTI emits a List as-is instead of emitting the individual elements from the array response.
      numKeys -
      keysAndArgs - must not be null.
      Returns:
      never null.
      See Also:
      • Redis Documentation: EVALSHA
